patterns.name: Hardcoded API Key
patterns.description: API key hardcoded in source code
patterns.recommendation: Move to environment variables or a secrets manager
patterns.fix_plan: Remove the inline API key assignment and load the key from runtime secret storage. Replace literals with env lookups (for example process.env.API_KEY or os.environ['API_KEY']) and add a startup validation that fails when missing.

patterns.name: Hardcoded Password
patterns.recommendation: Move passwords to environment variables or a secrets manager
patterns.fix_plan: Delete hardcoded password values from source and rotate credentials immediately. Inject passwords through secret managers or environment variables and update tests to use non-sensitive fixtures.

patterns.name: Hardcoded Secret
patterns.description: Secret or private key hardcoded in source code
patterns.recommendation: Store secrets in environment variables or a secrets manager
patterns.fix_plan: Replace hardcoded secret/private key literals with secret-manager references and rotate exposed material. Keep only key identifiers in code and fetch secrets at runtime with least-privilege access.

patterns.name: AWS Access Key
patterns.description: AWS access key detected in code
patterns.recommendation: Revoke and rotate the key, use environment variables
patterns.fix_plan: Immediately revoke and rotate the exposed AWS key and audit CloudTrail for abuse. Remove the key from source/history and supply credentials via IAM roles, OIDC federation, or secure environment injection.

patterns.name: GitHub Token
patterns.description: GitHub access token detected in code
patterns.recommendation: Revoke and rotate the token, use GitHub Secrets
patterns.fix_plan: Revoke the leaked token and issue a replacement with minimal scopes. Remove token literals from repo/workflows and reference credentials through GitHub Secrets or a short-lived OIDC flow.

patterns.name: JWT Token
patterns.recommendation: Do not commit tokens; use environment variables
patterns.fix_plan: Treat committed JWTs as compromised and invalidate the issuing session/keys if possible. Remove token literals from source and load test tokens from ephemeral fixtures or secure environment variables.

patterns.name: CORS Allow All with Credentials
patterns.description: CORS allows any origin with credentials enabled
patterns.recommendation: Restrict allowed origins when credentials are enabled
patterns.fix_plan: Set an explicit allowlist of trusted origins when credentials are true and reject all others. Replace wildcard/true origin settings with a function or list that validates exact production domains.

patterns.name: SQL Injection Risk
patterns.description: Potential SQL injection via unsafe query execution
patterns.recommendation: Use parameterized queries or safe query builders
patterns.fix_plan: Replace unsafe/raw SQL execution with parameterized query APIs and bound variables. Add a regression test using attacker-controlled input to confirm the query path no longer interpolates raw values.

patterns.name: dangerouslySetInnerHTML
patterns.description: Use of dangerouslySetInnerHTML can lead to XSS
patterns.recommendation: Sanitize HTML before rendering or avoid raw HTML
patterns.fix_plan: Avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML unless absolutely required; prefer escaped rendering. If needed, sanitize input with a vetted sanitizer (for example DOMPurify) and render only the sanitized variable.

patterns.name: eval() Usage
patterns.description: Use of eval can lead to code injection
patterns.recommendation: Avoid eval; use safe parsers or alternatives
patterns.fix_plan: Remove eval-based execution and replace with explicit parser/dispatch logic. For dynamic expressions, parse into a restricted AST or map known commands to safe handler functions.

patterns.name: Disabled SSL Verify
patterns.fix_plan: Enable certificate verification and remove verify=False/rejectUnauthorized:false in production code paths. Configure trusted CA bundles explicitly for internal services instead of disabling TLS checks.

patterns.name: Hardcoded IP/Host
patterns.description: Hardcoded IP address or host detected
patterns.recommendation: Move hosts to configuration or environment variables
patterns.fix_plan: Move endpoint hosts into environment-specific config and keep code host-agnostic. Define variables like API_BASE_URL per environment and reject direct literal IP endpoints in application logic.

patterns.name: SSRF Risk — User-Controlled URL Fetch
patterns.description: Server-side request using user-controlled URL without allowlist validation
patterns.message: Potential SSRF — user-controlled URL passed to server-side HTTP request
patterns.recommendation: Validate URLs against an allowlist of permitted hosts and block private/internal IPs
patterns.fix_plan: Parse the user-supplied URL and validate its host against an explicit allowlist before issuing the request. Block requests to private IP ranges (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 169.254.x, 127.x) and loopback addresses. Use DNS resolution pinning to prevent TOCTOU bypasses.

patterns.name: UUID/Internal ID Exposure in Response
patterns.description: Internal UUIDs or database IDs exposed in API responses without access control
patterns.message: Internal ID field exposed in response — may enable enumeration
patterns.recommendation: Use opaque external-facing IDs or ensure proper authorization before exposing internal identifiers
patterns.fix_plan: Map internal database IDs to opaque external identifiers (e.g., UUID v4 or hashid) before including them in API responses. Add authorization checks to ensure users can only access their own records.

patterns.name: PII Logged or Exposed
patterns.description: Personally identifiable information logged or included in error responses
patterns.message: PII field referenced in log statement — may leak to log aggregation
patterns.recommendation: Redact or mask PII before logging; never include raw PII in log output
patterns.fix_plan: Mask or redact PII fields before passing them to log statements. Use a structured logging library with automatic PII scrubbing. Replace raw field references with masked versions (e.g., email -> j***@example.com).

patterns.name: Admin Endpoint Without Auth Guard
patterns.description: Admin or privileged endpoint defined without authentication/authorization middleware
patterns.message: Admin route defined without visible auth guard — verify authorization is applied
patterns.recommendation: Ensure all /admin endpoints require authentication and admin-role authorization
patterns.fix_plan: Add authentication and admin-role authorization middleware to every /admin route. Use a centralized guard (e.g., require_admin dependency, isAdmin middleware) applied at the router or route level, not inline.
